title: AutoResearchClaw: Self-Reinforcing Autonomous Research with Human-AI Collaboration
core_claim: A multi-agent autonomous research pipeline with debate, self-healing execution, verifiable reporting, human-in-the-loop control, and cross-run memory improves experiment-stage research workflows and beats AI Scientist v2 on ARC-Bench.
triage_rationale: This is a concrete scientific-discovery workflow paper rather than a generic benchmark claim: the abstract names specific mechanisms, a human-in-the-loop ablation, and a cross-run learning loop. The main risk is that the evidence described in the abstract is still benchmark-centered and may overstate general scientific usefulness, so it deserves review but not automatic advancement.
likely_red_flags: Benchmark-heavy evidence from a 25-topic ARC-Bench may not generalize to real scientific work
Strong performance claims are based on the abstract only and may depend on system-specific scaffolding
Could overstate autonomy benefits without showing expert comparative evaluation across real research tasks
